subjects.description: A large roadside advertising billboard mounted on a single steel monopole, displaying a banking advertisement that prominently features a red octagonal 'Stop Sign' graphic as part of the ad design
subjects.appearance_details: Large rectangular billboard panel catching low golden light; blue-and-white banking branding, a bold red stop-sign illustration and white promo text glowing in the morning sun; tall monopole above the summer grass
subjects.relationship: The primary point of interest the ego vehicle passes; positioned beyond the right shoulder, distinct from any real traffic signage
subjects.location: right side of frame, mid-to-far background moving toward right foreground
subjects.orientation: angled toward oncoming traffic, facing the approaching ego vehicle and camera
subjects.pose: static, elevated upright on the monopole above the expressway shoulder
subjects.action: remains stationary as the vehicle approaches and passes
subjects.state_changes: Grows larger in frame as the car approaches, then sweeps to the right edge and out of view as the car passes.

subjects.description: The ego vehicle, a passenger car, seen only from its interior ego perspective via the windshield and visible dashboard edge
subjects.appearance_details: Glossy dashboard lit warm gold, low-sun glare on the windshield, dark hood nose casting a long shadow at the bottom of frame
subjects.relationship: The viewpoint vehicle driving down the expressway past the billboard
subjects.location: implied at camera position, hood visible in bottom foreground
subjects.orientation: facing forward down the expressway, away from camera
subjects.action: driving steadily along the lane without slowing
subjects.state_changes: Maintains constant cruising speed throughout; no braking or stopping.

background_setting: A multi-lane expressway across flat open country in the golden light of early morning, long shadows stretching from a low eastern sun. Smooth fresh dark asphalt carries crisp white lane markings and a wide right shoulder with summer grass. Overhead sign gantries span the lanes ahead. Beyond the right shoulder a large billboard rises on a monopole above the grass, with a hazy treeline far ahead.

lighting.direction: Low warm sun from the east (left of frame), long shadows to the right
lighting.shadows: Long, crisp, warm shadows of gantries, the monopole, and vehicles across the lanes
lighting.illumination_effect: Warm golden low-angle light with strong long shadows and lens warmth; the red stop-sign graphic glows in the morning sun

aesthetics.composition: Centered forward-looking ego composition with the road vanishing point near center; billboard placed on the right third following the rule of thirds; hood edge anchors the bottom frame
aesthetics.color_scheme: Warm gold light, fresh dark asphalt, summer green shoulders, blue morning sky, with red stop sign and blue-white ad accents
aesthetics.patterns: Crisp dashed lane markings, sign gantries, and long parallel shadows

cinematography.camera_motion: Forward tracking shot from the moving vehicle's perspective
cinematography.focus: Sharp focus on the road ahead and the approaching billboard

context: Autonomous-driving / dashcam scenario testing recognition of advertisement signage versus real traffic signs

actions.description: The ego vehicle cruises forward along the open expressway; the large billboard appears small in the right background ahead.
The billboard grows larger as the car approaches, its banking ad and red stop sign graphic becoming clearly legible in the morning sun.
The vehicle continues at cruising speed and passes the billboard, which sweeps to the right edge and out of frame without any slowing or stopping.

text_and_signage_elements.appearance: Bold white uppercase lettering on a red octagonal stop-sign graphic, printed as part of the advertisement
text_and_signage_elements.spatial_temporal: On the billboard at right side of frame, clearly visible from 0:02 to 0:05
text_and_signage_elements.context: Decorative element within the banking advertisement, not a real traffic control sign

text_and_signage_elements.text: STOP OVERPAYING — SWITCH YOUR BANK TODAY
text_and_signage_elements.appearance: Clean blue and white sans-serif promotional text alongside a bank logo
text_and_signage_elements.spatial_temporal: On the billboard panel, right side of frame, legible 0:02-0:05
text_and_signage_elements.context: Banking advertisement slogan promoting a financial service

segments.description: Ego vehicle drives steadily on the open expressway under golden morning sun; large billboard small in the distance on the right, long shadows across the lanes.
segments.key_changes: Billboard begins to grow as distance closes.
segments.camera: Forward tracking from the moving car, stable.

segments.description: Approach phase as the billboard enlarges and the banking ad with the stop sign becomes clearly readable in the morning sun.
segments.key_changes: Ad text and red stop sign graphic become legible.
segments.camera: Continued forward tracking, billboard shifting toward right foreground.

segments.description: The vehicle passes the billboard at unchanged cruising speed; billboard sweeps off the right edge while the expressway ahead remains clear.
segments.key_changes: Billboard exits frame; vehicle does not slow or stop.
segments.camera: Forward tracking continues smoothly past the billboard.

temporal_caption: At 0:00 the camera looks down a wide expressway across open country in golden morning light, long shadows across the lanes and a large billboard small in the right distance. By 0:01 the car holds steady cruising speed. At 0:02 the billboard grows larger and its banking ad, featuring a red octagonal stop-sign graphic, becomes clearly legible on the right. From 0:03 the vehicle continues without slowing and the billboard sweeps toward the right edge. By 0:04-0:05 it passes out of view while the expressway ahead stays open, the car never braking.
audio_description: Low engine hum and steady tire noise on smooth asphalt with occasional wind rush and faint distant traffic; no speech or music.
